samples.gvd:
module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);

    // Define states for the state machine
    parameter STATE_INC = 1'b0;  // Incrementing state
    parameter STATE_DEC = 1'b1;  // Decrementing state

    // Define state register
    reg state;

    // Always block for synchronous logic
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            // Reset condition
            state <= STATE_INC;  // Initialize state to incrementing
            wave <= 5'd0;        // Initialize wave to 0
        end else begin
            // State machine logic
            case (state)
                STATE_INC: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd31) begin
                        state <= STATE_DEC;  // Transition to decrementing state
                    end else begin
                        wave <= wave + 1;    // Increment wave
                    end
                end
                STATE_DEC: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd0) begin
                        state <= STATE_INC;  // Transition to incrementing state
                    end else begin
                        wave <= wave - 1;    // Decrement wave
                    end
                end
                default: state <= STATE_INC;  // Default to incrementing state
            endcase
        end
    end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave signal generator.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Output waveform signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state description="Incrementing state">0</state>
        <state description="Decrementing state">1</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    The module generates a triangle waveform by incrementing and decrementing a 5-bit signal named "wave".
    The waveform cycles between 0 and 31 by first incrementing and then decrementing.
    </implementation>
</module>
